text: pivoted back toward his scattered enemies, emptying his rifle into them . The soldiers who’d advanced from the Clinic with him did the same from the other side. Their gunfire drowned out all other sounds. When it ended, a heavy silence settled over the area . Only two of the bots moved. A gearhead reached a single hand forward, smoke curling out of its perforated casing, and dug its fingers into the dirt. Actuators whining, it dragged itself forward an inch . Ronin focused on the other movement, knowing somehow which of them it would be. He tossed the assault rifle aside as Warlord, impossibly, pushed up onto his knees. One of his optics was a gaping hole, and the synthetic flesh had separated entirely from the bottom of his face. Bullet holes perforated his body, and his clothes were in tatters . “You’re supposed to be dead,” Warlord said. His jaw fell open with the first word and ceased its motion . “From what I’ve learned, so are you, Kevin,” Ronin replied, swinging the AMR into his hands. It was a battle to keep from turning around to find Lara. He needed to see her, to know she was all right, but the possibility that she wasn’t … Warlord snickered, shaking his head. “Haven’t heard that name in years .” The crowd moved in, slowly surrounding the pile of deactivated bots. Their anger charged the air with static. No one spoke. Warlord ran his remaining optic over them, his exposed face plates oddly neutral in their positioning . “It was yours, long ago. Kevin Turner. When you were human .” A gasp ran through the humans, and the entire crowd murmured to one another. Warlord remained still, his lone eye on Ronin again . “That supposed to startle me?” Warlord asked. “Supposed to…jar my memory into overload ?” “You had a family,” Ronin persisted. “People who cared for you .” Where was Lara? Despite the damage done to Warlord, Ronin couldn’t look away, couldn’t trust him . “Diane, Michael, and Liam,” Warlord responded . “You remember them now ?” “I never forgot. Never forgot that humans and their war did this to the world, that it was mankind’s fault I never got to see them again, to hold them or touch them or hear their voices!” He swung an arm forward, throwing himself off-balance, and caught himself with his other hand . “Did you forget the fear you put in Diane’s eyes, the last time you saw her ?” Warlord remained hunched over, propped on his arm, head down. Fluids dripped from the open damage on his head and body, too much like blood. “Do you believe in destiny, dustwalker? Logic says it’s all just a game of probability, but for all these pieces to fall into place …” “You don’t think this is result of cause and effect? The consequences of your actions finally come to fruition ?” “I should. But there’s more here. The masterless warrior seeking to right the wrongs done by the local warlord, rallying the townsfolk to stand against him…it’s too much like an old story to be anything but destiny that brought you to Cheyenne .” There was movement in the crowd behind Warlord. Lara pushed her way to the front of the ring. Strands of hair stuck out from her braid, and her face was smudged with dirt. There were small cuts on her cheeks and arms, and a larger gash near her shoulder. Crimson stained her tattered clothing near her ribs . But her expression was strong, her eyes determined. She offered Ronin a small, relieved smile, and dropped her attention to Warlord . “I believe in fate,” Ronin said . “You’re everything they’re not, dustwalker. They destroyed this world. They don’t deserve it anymore. We’re stronger, faster, tougher, smarter. We’re immortal . You’ll see it yourself, eventually. We are the future, and they’re nothing but a lingering curiosity .” “But you’re the one everyone is staring at, like a creature in a cage,” Ronin said . Warlord lifted his head, meeting Ronin’s gaze. Seconds ticked by. The plates around Warlord’s mouth shifted subtly, and he threw himself upright, rifle in hand . A single gunshot went off, and Warlord’s remaining optic exploded outward. Warlord’s rifle fired into the grass, his body pitching forward, and he sagged to the ground . Lara stood behind him, pistol raised in both hands, her brows low . “Fuck you,” she spat . Warlord laughed, struggling to rise on trembling arms. “Already did that, didn’t you ?” Ronin stepped forward, slamming a boot down onto Warlord’s back to force him into the dirt. He fired the last round from the AMR. Its boom echoed across Cheyenne and into the Dust, lingering for five seconds in the silence. Blue fire poured from Warlord, sprouting and extinguishing in a flash as his power cell burned up . Dropping the rifle, Ronin stopped the automated diagnostics, cancelled the alerts informing him of the damage he’d taken. He turned toward Lara, intending to go to her, but she was already there . She threw her arms around him in a desperate embrace. Ronin returned it, burying his face in her hair . Warlord’s reign had ended, and Lara was alive, in his arms. Her heat seeped into his skin, lighting up his sensors. The chances that he’d never feel her again, never hold her again, had been so high … Lara pulled back, taking his cheeks between her hands. Her gaze swept over his face. Tears flooded her eyes, but she smiled. “You look like shit .” Ronin smiled back. “And you look beautiful .” She laughed, tears falling before she pressed her mouth against his. Her lips lingered, caressing his, even as a sob escaped her . “I knew you’d be fine,” she said . “I had the best reason to survive .” “All right everyone,” Cooper called, claiming the crowd’s attention. “We’re clearing the rubble away from
